goal: `$ yaptap` records from the microphone until Enter is pressed, then prints the Whisper transcript to stdout.

in_scope: Phase 1 error handling and exit codes

tasks.title: Create Cargo manifest for the `yaptap` binary
Add phase 1 Rust dependencies: cpal, hound, tempfile, anyhow, ctrlc, tracing, tracing-subscriber
Create `src/main.rs` with a `main()` entrypoint that returns `anyhow::Result<()>`
Initialize tracing subscriber in `main` with `RUST_LOG` support
Start microphone capture immediately when `yaptap` launches
Print `Recording... (press Enter to stop)` when capture starts
Implement an in-place elapsed-time counter line using carriage return updates
Resolve default input device via `cpal::default_host().default_input_device()`
Print `error: no input device found` to stderr and exit code 1 when no microphone is available
Negotiate an input stream config targeting 16 kHz mono i16 (or closest supported)
Capture PCM samples in a shared in-memory `Vec<i16>` from the CPAL callback
Create a dedicated stdin thread that blocks on `read_line()` for Enter detection
Send an mpsc stop signal from the stdin thread when Enter is pressed
Stop and close the input stream when the Enter stop signal is received
Create a temp WAV path named `yaptap_<timestamp>.wav` using `tempfile::Builder`
Encode captured samples into WAV using `hound::WavWriter` with negotiated format
Finalize and flush WAV output before launching the Python transcription subprocess
Register a SIGINT handler that deletes temp WAV files and exits with code 130
Delete temp WAV files after successful transcription output
Delete temp WAV files on runtime failures before process exit
Validate `python3` is available on PATH before launching transcription
Print `error: python3 not found` to stderr and exit code 1 when `python3` is unavailable
Validate `ffmpeg` is available on PATH before launching the transcription subprocess
Print `error: ffmpeg not found` to stderr and exit code 1 when `ffmpeg` is unavailable
Print `Transcribing...` after recording stops and before running the subprocess
Spawn `python3 src/core/transcribe.py <wav_path>` and capture stdout/stderr
Print `error: transcription failed — <subprocess stderr>` to stderr and exit code 1 on non-zero subprocess exit
Print transcript stdout from subprocess as newline-terminated CLI output
Exit with code 0 when recording, transcription, and output complete successfully
Create `src/__init__.py` as an empty file to make `src` a Python package
Create `src/core/__init__.py` as an empty file to make `src.core` a Python package
Scaffold `src/core/transcribe.py` with module-level docstring, imports (`argparse`, `logging`, `sys`, `pathlib`, `whisper`), and empty function stubs for `transcribe()` and `main()`
Add AI intent comment block for `transcribe()` purpose and invariants
Validate `wav_path` is non-empty in `transcribe()` and raise `ValueError` otherwise
Validate WAV file existence in `transcribe()` and raise `ValueError` when missing
Load Whisper model in `transcribe()` and wrap load failures as `RuntimeError`
Run `model.transcribe()` and return stripped transcript text (allow empty string)
Parse `wav_path` and optional `--model` argument (default `base`) in Python CLI
Print `error: <message>` to stderr and exit code 1 for Python transcription failures
Print transcript to stdout in Python CLI on success
Create `src/core/transcribe_test.py` using Python `unittest`
Mock `whisper.load_model` and model transcription calls in unit tests
Add test: valid WAV returns transcript text
Add test: empty `wav_path` raises `ValueError`
Add test: missing WAV file raises `ValueError`
Add test: Whisper model load failure raises `RuntimeError`
Add test: Whisper transcription failure raises `RuntimeError`
Document phase 1 transcription test command: `python -m unittest src.core.transcribe_test`
Verify `cargo install --path .` produces an installable `yaptap` binary
